wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for QwenEcho focused on quick setup and first-time usage. Explain installation requirements for Android (API 30+, arm64-v8a) and iOS (16+, arm64), including minimum hardware specifications (4GB+ RAM recommended). Document the complete model provisioning process, covering GGUF/INT4 model downloads (~2.6GB total space required) for ASR (FunASR-Nano ~150MB), LLM (Qwen3-4B-Instruct ~2.2GB), and TTS (Qwen3-TTS-Streaming ~250MB). Provide step-by-step build instructions for both native engine (CMake) and Flutter app. Include initial app configuration, language selection, and basic usage walkthrough for face-to-face interpretation. Address common setup issues and provide troubleshooting guidance for platform-specific problems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Engine Manager
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the EngineManager component focused on engine lifecycle management and state machine orchestration. Explain the five-state state machine (Uninitialized → Initializing → Ready → Running → Stopping) with detailed transition rules and error conditions. Document the three-phase initialization process including model loading, validation, and resource allocation. Detail session management patterns for starting and stopping interpretation pipelines with language pair configuration. Include error handling strategies for model loading failures, unsupported languages, and concurrent access prevention. Provide examples of proper engine lifecycle usage patterns, resource cleanup procedures, and integration with the Flutter layer through FFI bindings.

wiki_catalogs.name: Split View Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the SplitView component that implements QwenEcho's bilateral interpretation interface. Explain the 50/50 screen division with 180-degree rotation for face-to-face conversation scenarios. Document the state management approach using GlobalKey for independent speaker half control, orientation locking to portrait mode, and immersive system UI handling. Detail the message routing methods (addAsrPartial, addAsrConfirmed, addTranslation) that push content to specific speaker halves based on speaker IDs. Include examples of widget composition patterns, lifecycle management in initState/dispose, and integration with SpeakerHalf components. Address responsive layout considerations and cross-platform compatibility.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for QwenEcho's system design focused on the hybrid Flutter-native architecture. Explain the high-level separation between Flutter UI shell and C/C++ native engine, detailing how this design enables real-time performance while maintaining cross-platform compatibility. Document the four-function FFI interface pattern (initialize, process_audio, shutdown, get_status) and its role in minimizing Dart-to-native communication overhead. Describe the message-based communication system using Dart Ports for asynchronous event delivery from native to Flutter. Include architectural diagrams showing component boundaries, data flow patterns, and the clear separation of concerns between presentation layer (Flutter) and processing layer (Native). Address design principles like offline-first operation, zero-knowledge architecture, and platform abstraction that enable air-gapped deployment.

wiki_catalogs.name: Audio Collection and Capture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for QwenEcho's audio collection system focused on platform-independent microphone capture. Explain the AudioCollector's real-time priority thread implementation with AAudio (Android) and AVAudioEngine (iOS) integration. Document the 16kHz, 16-bit, mono audio format configuration and sample drop detection mechanisms. Detail the HAL abstraction layer that provides unified audio capture interfaces across platforms. Include examples of configuring audio parameters, handling audio interruptions, managing audio permissions, and optimizing latency for real-time performance. Address thread priority management, buffer synchronization strategies, and error recovery patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: HAL Architecture Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for QwenEcho's HAL architecture design principles. Explain the unified interface pattern used across all platform abstractions, including common patterns like opaque handles, callback-based APIs, and error handling strategies. Document the design decisions behind choosing C-linkage interfaces, memory management conventions, and thread safety guarantees. Include examples of how new HAL components should be structured and integrated into the system.

wiki_catalogs.name: ASR Stage - Speech Recognition
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the ASR (Automatic Speech Recognition) stage focused on speech-to-text conversion using FunASR-Nano. Explain the stage's architecture including worker thread processing, HAL accelerator integration with NPU-first and CPU fallback mechanisms, and streaming partial token output. Document the thermal mode system with Normal (16kHz inference) and Throttle (8kHz resampling) modes. Detail the SLA requirements of ≤200ms first-character latency and how the stage enforces this through performance monitoring. Include examples of creating ASR instances, processing locked segments, handling partial vs confirmed text outputs, and integrating with the sentence segmenter. Address noise-only segment filtering, error handling strategies, and resource management patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Thermal Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for QwenEcho's thermal management system focused on the three-mode thermal state machine. Explain the temperature thresholds (43°C throttle, 50°C critical) and hysteresis behavior with specific transition rules: Normal→Throttle at >43°C, Throttle→Normal at ≤42°C, Throttle→Critical at >50°C, Critical→Throttle at ≤45°C. Document the ThermalMode enum (THERMAL_NORMAL, THERMAL_THROTTLE, THERMAL_CRITICAL) and the callback mechanism for engine adaptation. Detail the low-priority polling thread that runs every 5 seconds and the MSG_THERMAL_STATE notification to the UI shell. Include examples of implementing custom thermal callbacks, configuring temperature thresholds, and handling thermal state transitions in the Engine Manager.

wiki_catalogs.name: GGUF Model Format
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the GGUF model format used in QwenEcho. Explain the GGUF file structure including magic bytes (0x46475547), header fields, tensor storage, and metadata key-value pairs. Document the INT4 quantization formats (Q4_0, Q4_1, Q4_K) and their memory layout optimizations. Detail the validation procedures for model integrity checking, version compatibility, and quantization type verification. Include examples of GGUF file parsing, header validation, and error handling for corrupted or incompatible models. Address performance considerations for different quantization levels and their impact on accuracy vs. memory usage trade-offs.

wiki_catalogs.name: C FFI Interface Specification
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the C FFI interface exposed to Dart. Document all four C-linkage entry points: InitQwenEchoEngine, StartEchoPipeline, StopEchoPipeline, and RegisterEchoMessagePort. For each function, specify parameter types, return values, error codes (EchoErrorCode), and usage constraints. Include detailed examples of proper initialization sequences, error handling patterns, and resource management. Document the visibility attributes and extern "C" linkage requirements. Provide guidance on extending the interface with additional functions while maintaining ABI compatibility.

wiki_catalogs.name: C FFI API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive C FFI API documentation for QwenEcho's native interface. Document all four C-linkage functions: InitQwenEchoEngine with model path parameters and GGUF file requirements, StartEchoPipeline with ISO 639-1 language codes and pipeline activation, StopEchoPipeline with resource cleanup behavior, and RegisterEchoMessagePort for Dart communication setup. Include complete parameter specifications, return code enumerations (EchoErrorCode), error handling patterns, and thread safety considerations. Provide concrete usage examples showing proper initialization sequences, error recovery strategies, and memory management best practices. Document the visibility attributes and compilation requirements for cross-platform deployment.

wiki_catalogs.name: Pipeline Lifecycle Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the PipelineController lifecycle management focused on creation, initialization, and destruction of the audio processing pipeline. Explain the create-start-stop-destroy pattern with proper resource management semantics. Document the graceful shutdown sequence that ensures no data loss during pipeline termination, including the 2-second timeout constraint. Detail error handling strategies for each lifecycle phase and state validation mechanisms. Include examples of proper resource cleanup patterns and common lifecycle errors with their resolution steps.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for QwenEcho's core design patterns. Describe the layered architecture separating Flutter UI from native processing, the pipeline pattern for audio processing stages, and the observer pattern for message-based communication. Document the state machine pattern used in engine management and thermal monitoring. Explain how these patterns work together to achieve real-time performance while maintaining clean separation of concerns. Include component interaction diagrams showing data flow between layers and the clear boundaries between presentation, business logic, and processing layers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Facade Pattern Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for QwenEcho's facade pattern implementation focusing on the EchoEngine class that combines NativeBridge and PortManager responsibilities. Thoroughly explain the lifecycle management from initialization through running state to disposal. Document the three-state machine (uninitialized, ready, running) and state transitions. Detail how the facade abstracts complex native operations behind simple Dart APIs. Include concrete examples of engine instantiation with both default and custom bridges, dependency injection patterns for testing, and error propagation strategies. Address the separation of concerns between UI layer and native engine communication.

wiki_catalogs.name: Thermal Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for QwenEcho's thermal management system focused on the three-mode thermal state machine implementation. Detail the Normal → Throttle → Critical state transitions with hysteresis thresholds (43°C, 42°C, 50°C, 45°C). Document the thermal monitoring thread architecture running at 5-second intervals and low priority scheduling. Explain the callback mechanism for engine adaptation and Native Port messaging to UI shell. Include concrete examples of thermal policy implementation, platform-specific temperature polling via HAL, and adaptive throttling strategies. Address thermal state persistence, recovery mechanisms, and user feedback through status indicators. Provide troubleshooting guidance for thermal-related performance issues and optimization techniques for different device classes.

wiki_catalogs.name: BoundedSPSCQueue Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the BoundedSPSCQueue component focused on lock-free bounded queue implementation with overflow-drop semantics. Explain the slot-based architecture with sequence numbers for occupancy tracking, power-of-two capacity requirements for bitmask indexing, and the producer-consumer concurrency model. Document the try_push() method's overflow handling strategy where oldest elements are dropped when full, and try_pop() method's atomic claim mechanism using compare_exchange operations. Detail memory ordering strategies (acquire/release/acq_rel) and cache line alignment to prevent false sharing. Include examples of template usage, capacity configuration, and performance characteristics under different load scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Ring Buffer Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for QwenEcho's AudioRingBuffer implementation focused on lock-free SPSC circular buffer design. Explain the power-of-two capacity optimization using bitmask operations for efficient modulo calculations. Document the atomic memory ordering strategy with acquire/release semantics for thread safety between producer and consumer threads. Detail the overflow handling mechanism that advances the read pointer to overwrite oldest samples, ensuring zero-blocking behavior for real-time audio processing. Include specific examples of write/read operations, wrap-around handling, and cache-line alignment techniques to prevent false sharing. Address performance characteristics, memory layout optimization, and integration patterns with the audio pipeline stages.

wiki_catalogs.name: FFI Interface Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for QwenEcho's FFI interface design focused on the four-function C-linkage API. Document each function (InitQwenEchoEngine, StartEchoPipeline, StopEchoPipeline, RegisterEchoMessagePort) including parameters, return values, error codes, and usage patterns. Explain the design rationale for minimizing Dart-to-native communication overhead through batch operations. Include examples of proper initialization sequences, error handling strategies, and resource management across the FFI boundary. Detail the memory safety considerations and type marshaling between Dart and C/C++.

wiki_catalogs.name: Audio HAL Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Audio HAL interface focused on the unified cross-platform microphone capture API. Document the AudioCapture opaque handle structure and its complete lifecycle: create, start, stop, and destroy methods. Detail the callback-based architecture with hal_audio_capture_callback_t function signature, explaining the real-time audio thread context and performance constraints. Include sample rate configuration options, channel management (mono/stereo), error handling patterns, and memory safety considerations. Provide usage examples showing proper initialization, callback implementation, and resource cleanup. Address threading model, buffer size considerations, and integration patterns with the higher-level audio processing pipeline.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for QwenEcho's hybrid Flutter-native architecture. Describe the high-level system design showing the separation between Flutter UI shell and C/C++ native engine. Document the four-function FFI interface pattern and Dart Port message system for inter-process communication. Explain the audio processing pipeline architecture with lock-free SPSC ring buffers and cascade truncation strategy. Detail the three-mode thermal state machine design and platform abstraction layer (HAL) for cross-platform compatibility. Include system context diagrams showing component interactions, data flows between stages (Audio Collector → Ring Buffer → VAD/Segmenter → ASR → LLM → TTS), and performance optimization strategies. Address architectural decisions, trade-offs, and constraints that enable real-time bilateral translation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Pipeline Controller
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the PipelineController component focused on orchestrating the sequential audio processing pipeline. Explain how the controller manages the producer-consumer chain from AudioCollector through ASR, LLM, and TTS stages. Document the lock-free queue system used for inter-stage communication and thread synchronization mechanisms. Detail the stage instantiation pattern, parameter passing between stages, and error propagation throughout the pipeline. Include monitoring capabilities for pipeline health, latency tracking, and performance metrics collection. Provide examples of extending the pipeline with custom processing stages and debugging pipeline bottlenecks.

wiki_catalogs.name: Speaker Half Component
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the SpeakerHalf component that represents individual speaker interfaces in the split view. Thoroughly explain the component's role as a container for text display and user interaction within each speaker's designated area. Document the speaker ID assignment, state management for line updates, and integration with the TextDisplay component. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing how ASR partial/confirmed text and translations are rendered. Document configuration options, styling customization, and accessibility features. Address performance considerations for real-time text updates and memory management for large conversation histories.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for QwenEcho's component architecture focusing on the modular design and inter-component relationships. Thoroughly explain the EchoEngine facade pattern in Flutter that combines NativeBridge and PortManager responsibilities. Document the EngineManager state machine in the native layer managing initialization, session lifecycle, and resource cleanup. Detail the PipelineController orchestration of audio processing stages with lock-free queues. Explain the NativePort system for asynchronous message delivery and the typed event system using EchoMessage hierarchy. Include concrete examples of component instantiation, dependency injection patterns, and error propagation strategies. Address component isolation, testing strategies, and extension points for adding new processing stages or platform implementations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Lock-free Ring Buffer
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for QwenEcho's lock-free circular ring buffer implementation designed for zero-contention audio streaming. Explain the producer-consumer architecture where AudioCollector writes samples while consumers read without blocking. Document the memory layout, atomic operations, and cache-friendly design patterns used to achieve high-performance audio data transfer. Detail the buffer capacity management, overflow handling, and underflow detection mechanisms. Include examples of configuring buffer sizes, monitoring fill levels, and optimizing throughput for different audio workloads. Address memory alignment considerations, cross-platform compatibility, and debugging techniques for buffer-related issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Audio HAL Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Audio HAL component focused on cross-platform microphone capture. Document the unified AudioCapture interface with create/start/stop/destroy lifecycle methods. Detail the Android implementation using AAudio low-latency mode for real-time audio processing, including buffer management and callback handling. Explain the iOS implementation using AVAudioEngine input taps for microphone access. Include sample rate configuration, channel management, error handling, and performance considerations for real-time audio processing.

wiki_catalogs.name: LLM Stage - Language Translation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the LLM (Large Language Model) stage focused on bilingual translation capabilities using Qwen3-4B-Instruct. Explain the context window management system including cascade truncation for maintaining conversation history while respecting memory constraints. Document the streaming token output mechanism that enables real-time translation display. Detail the integration with ASR input queue and TTS output queue, including data structure transformations between AsrToLlmElement and LlmToTtsElement formats. Include examples of configuring language pairs, managing context windows, handling long conversations, and optimizing translation latency. Address error recovery, model loading strategies, and performance tuning parameters for different device capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Memory Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for QwenEcho's memory management system focused on progressive cleanup strategies and resource optimization. Explain the two-level intervention system: Level 1 (KV cache release) for moderate memory pressure and Level 2 (pipeline stop) for critical situations. Document the Memory Monitor's RSS monitoring capabilities and automatic cleanup triggers. Detail the integration with the offline policy system and how memory constraints affect model loading and processing stages. Include examples of configuring memory budgets, monitoring memory usage patterns, implementing custom cleanup strategies, and responding to memory pressure events through the callback system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Model Loader Core
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Model Loader core functionality focused on GGUF model loading and validation. Thoroughly explain the model_loader_load() function's multi-step validation process including file existence checks, permission verification, magic byte validation, and quantization format verification. Document the memory-mapped file access via mmap for OS page cache optimization. Detail the inference context creation and management for ASR, LLM, and TTS models. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing proper model loading workflows, error handling strategies, and resource cleanup patterns. Document the ModelInfo structure for per-model status reporting and memory consumption tracking.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dart FFI Bindings Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Dart FFI bindings implementation that wraps the native C interface. Explain how Dart FFI is used to call native functions, handle memory management across language boundaries, and manage type conversions between Dart and C types. Document the NativeBridge class structure, error handling strategies for FFI calls, and lifecycle management of native resources. Include examples of proper async/await patterns when calling native functions, exception handling for native errors, and debugging techniques for FFI communication issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dart Facade API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed Dart facade API documentation for QwenEcho's high-level interface. Document the EchoEngine class methods for engine lifecycle management including initialization with model paths, pipeline control with language pair configuration, and graceful shutdown procedures. Detail the NativeBridge wrapper methods that handle FFI calls and error translation. Document the PortManager for asynchronous message handling and event streaming. Provide practical examples demonstrating common usage patterns like starting interpretation sessions, handling real-time messages, and managing engine state transitions. Address Flutter-specific considerations like widget integration and UI updates from background operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Stage Instantiation and Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the stage instantiation system focused on how the PipelineController creates and configures individual processing stages (AudioCollector, SentenceSegmenter, ASR, LLM, TTS). Explain the factory pattern used for stage creation, parameter passing between stages, and dependency injection mechanisms. Document the configuration options available for each stage type and how language pair validation affects stage initialization. Include examples of extending the pipeline with custom stages and debugging stage initialization failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Native Port System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for QwenEcho's native port system focusing on asynchronous message delivery between native C++ and Dart layers. Document the typed event system using EchoMessage hierarchy for type-safe communication. Detail the producer-consumer pattern implementation for handling high-frequency audio processing events. Include concrete examples of message creation, sending, and receiving patterns. Address thread safety considerations, memory management across FFI boundaries, and performance optimization strategies for real-time messaging.

wiki_catalogs.name: Memory Optimization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for QwenEcho's memory optimization strategies focused on progressive cleanup mechanisms and RSS monitoring. Thoroughly explain the memory budget enforcement system with automatic model cache eviction and resource cleanup triggers. Document the memory pressure detection algorithms and response strategies including model unloading, buffer resizing, and temporary file cleanup. Include concrete examples of memory monitoring implementation using platform-specific APIs (Android Process.getMemoryInfo, iOS process_info). Detail the integration with model loader for dynamic context window adjustment and the relationship between thermal states and memory policies. Address memory leak prevention, allocation tracking, and performance impact analysis. Provide benchmarking methodologies for memory usage patterns and optimization recommendations for different device memory constraints.

wiki_catalogs.name: AudioRingBuffer Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the AudioRingBuffer component focused on circular buffer implementation optimized for real-time audio processing. Explain the ring buffer architecture designed for continuous audio data streaming, memory management strategies for large audio buffers, and thread synchronization mechanisms for concurrent read/write operations. Document buffer wrapping behavior, overflow handling, and integration with platform-specific audio APIs. Detail performance optimizations including memory alignment, zero-copy operations where possible, and efficient memory access patterns. Include examples of buffer initialization, audio data writing/reading operations, and monitoring buffer utilization for quality assurance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Bounded SPSC Queues
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed content for QwenEcho's bounded single-producer single-consumer queue system used for inter-stage communication in the audio processing pipeline. Explain the queue architecture designed for transferring processed data between ASR, LLM, and TTS stages with backpressure handling. Document the bounded capacity strategy that prevents unbounded memory growth during high-load scenarios. Detail the enqueue/dequeue operations with proper synchronization mechanisms and error handling for full/empty conditions. Address queue monitoring, utilization metrics, and integration patterns with the overall pipeline controller.

wiki_catalogs.name: Typed Message Protocol
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for QwenEcho's typed message protocol system. Document all message types including AsrPartialMessage, TranslationStreamMessage, SystemStatusMessage, and their corresponding native counterparts. Explain the message structure, serialization format, and field definitions. Include examples of message creation, parsing, and routing patterns. Detail the event-driven architecture that enables real-time streaming from native engine to Flutter UI. Address message ordering guarantees, backpressure handling, and error propagation mechanisms.

Explain the integration with offline policy for adaptive quality adjustment based on measured latencies. Address latency reporting mechanisms, alerting systems for performance degradation, and debugging tools for latency bottleneck identification. Provide performance baseline establishment and regression detection approaches for continuous performance monitoring.

wiki_catalogs.name: Sentence Segmentation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for QwenEcho's sentence segmentation system focused on voice activity detection and speech boundary identification. Explain the algorithm for detecting silence periods, phoneme transitions, and natural speech breaks to segment continuous audio streams into processable units. Document the LockedSegment data structure containing audio chunks with metadata like timestamps, speaker identification, and confidence scores. Detail the integration with the audio ring buffer for consuming raw PCM samples and producing segmented audio chunks for ASR processing. Include configuration parameters for sensitivity thresholds, minimum segment duration, and overlap handling. Address performance considerations for real-time processing and accuracy trade-offs in noisy environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Native Port Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Native Port management system that handles asynchronous message delivery from C++ to Dart. Explain the port registration lifecycle, thread-safe message queuing, and cross-platform implementation differences. Document the PortManager class architecture, message routing algorithms, and connection state management. Include examples of port setup, message sending patterns, and cleanup procedures. Address performance considerations, memory management, and debugging techniques for inter-process communication issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: iOS Audio Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the iOS audio implementation focused on AVAudioEngine input tap integration. Document the iOS-specific AudioCapture structure and AVAudioEngine setup for microphone access. Explain the input tap callback mechanism, including AVAudioPCMBuffer handling, sample rate conversion, and channel management. Detail the Objective-C bridge layer between Swift/Objective-C audio APIs and the C HAL interface. Include iOS-specific configuration for low-latency audio, session management, and background audio handling. Address iOS audio session categories, interruption handling, and device orientation changes. Cover memory management patterns, ARC considerations, and performance optimization for real-time audio processing on iOS devices. Provide debugging strategies using Instruments and Xcode audio tools.

wiki_catalogs.name: Native Engine Core
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for QwenEcho's C/C++ native engine core focused on the high-performance audio processing pipeline. Explain the EngineManager state machine for lifecycle management and session control. Document the PipelineController orchestration of sequential processing stages with lock-free communication. Detail the NativePort system for asynchronous Dart-to-native message delivery and the EchoTypes shared data structures. Include error handling strategies, resource management patterns, and threading models used throughout the engine. Provide examples of extending the pipeline with custom processing stages and integrating new AI models. Address performance considerations, memory management, and debugging techniques for the native layer.

wiki_catalogs.name: Echo Types System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the EchoTypes system focused on shared data structures and type definitions across the native engine. Document core enums like EngineState, EchoErrorCode, and ProcessingStage with their valid values and usage contexts. Explain complex data structures such as LockedSegment, AsrToLlmElement, and LlmToTtsElement used for inter-stage communication. Detail memory layout considerations, alignment requirements, and lifetime management for shared data. Include examples of proper data structure usage, validation patterns, and conversion utilities. Address compatibility considerations for different platforms and threading implications of shared data access.

wiki_catalogs.name: Status Monitoring
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the StatusBar component that provides real-time system health monitoring and user feedback. Thoroughly explain the offline indicator display, thermal status visualization, and system resource monitoring integration. Document the connection between Flutter UI and native engine status through EchoEngine facade. Include concrete examples of status updates, color coding for different states (normal, warning, critical), and user notification patterns. Document configuration options for status indicators, custom status messages, and accessibility features for screen readers. Address performance considerations for frequent status updates and battery impact optimization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Performance Optimization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for QwenEcho's performance optimization strategies focused on real-time mobile AI processing constraints. Thoroughly explain the three-mode thermal state machine (Normal, Warm, Hot) with adaptive throttling mechanisms and automatic model quality adjustment. Document the progressive memory cleanup strategy with RSS monitoring and budget enforcement. Detail the end-to-end latency tracking system measuring time from audio capture to speech output. Include concrete examples of thermal policy implementation, memory pressure response algorithms, and performance degradation prevention. Address CPU/GPU utilization optimization, cache management strategies, and platform-specific optimizations for Android and iOS. Provide benchmarking methodologies and performance regression detection approaches.

wiki_catalogs.name: Bounded SPSC Queues
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for QwenEcho's bounded single-producer single-consumer (SPSC) queue system used for inter-stage communication in the audio pipeline. Explain the lock-free queue implementation with fixed capacity bounds and backpressure mechanisms. Document the AsrToLlmElement and LlmToTtsElement data structures that flow through these queues between processing stages. Detail the queue lifecycle management, memory allocation strategies, and overflow handling when producers outpace consumers. Include examples of configuring queue sizes, monitoring queue depths, and implementing proper cleanup procedures. Address thread safety guarantees, performance characteristics, and debugging approaches for queue-related bottlenecks.

wiki_catalogs.name: Memory HAL Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Memory HAL component focused on process memory monitoring and management. Document the RSS (Resident Set Size) monitoring interface and memory pressure detection APIs. Detail the Android implementation using /proc/self/statm and the iOS implementation using mach_vm APIs. Include memory budget enforcement, progressive cleanup strategies, and integration with the offline policy system for automatic resource management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Stage Communication and Data Flow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for inter-stage communication mechanisms in the AI processing pipeline. Explain the bounded SPSC (Single Producer Single Consumer) queue system used for lock-free data transfer between ASR→LLM and LLM→TTS stages. Document the data structures including LockedSegment, AsrToLlmElement, and LlmToTtsElement with their field definitions and lifecycle management. Detail the message flow patterns, backpressure handling, and memory safety guarantees provided by the queue implementations. Include examples of implementing custom stages, handling queue overflow scenarios, and debugging data flow issues. Address performance considerations including queue sizing, memory allocation strategies, and thread synchronization patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Offline Policy System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for QwenEcho's offline policy system focused on compile-time symbol poisoning and runtime verification to prevent network access. Explain the security architecture that ensures zero-knowledge operation by blocking all network APIs at both compilation and runtime levels. Document the symbol poisoning mechanism that prevents accidental inclusion of networking libraries and the runtime checks that verify network isolation. Detail the policy enforcement points throughout the codebase and how the system maintains air-gapped operation guarantees. Include examples of verifying offline compliance, testing network isolation, and extending the policy system for additional security constraints.

wiki_catalogs.name: Model Catalog and Repository
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the Flutter-side model catalog and repository system. Explain the ModelCatalog's role in managing model metadata, versions, and availability across ASR, LLM, and TTS components. Document the ModelRepository's file-based storage management, model discovery mechanisms, and update workflows. Detail the Dart API for querying available models, checking model integrity, and managing model lifecycles from the UI layer. Include examples of adding new language packs, implementing model update checks, and handling model migration scenarios. Address caching strategies for model metadata and offline-first design patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Typed Message Protocol
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the typed event system used for inter-process communication between native engine and Flutter UI. Detail the EchoMessage class hierarchy, message serialization formats, and type-safe event handling patterns. Document all supported message types including pipeline status updates, transcription results, translation outputs, and error notifications. Explain the message routing mechanisms, event listener registration, and subscription patterns. Include examples of implementing custom message handlers and extending the protocol with new message types.

wiki_catalogs.name: Thread Synchronization and Coordination
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for thread synchronization mechanisms focused on coordinating multiple pipeline threads safely and efficiently. Explain the threading model used by the pipeline controller, including thread creation, priority management, and lifecycle coordination. Document synchronization primitives used for inter-thread communication, condition variables, and atomic operations. Detail the thread safety guarantees provided by the pipeline and best practices for extending the threading model. Include examples of adding new threads to the pipeline and debugging thread-related issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Communication Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for QwenEcho's component communication patterns focusing on the typed event system and inter-process messaging protocols. Thoroughly explain the EchoMessage hierarchy design and how it enables type-safe communication between Flutter UI and native engine components. Document the FFI bridge implementation details including function marshaling, error code translation, and data serialization. Detail the observer pattern implementation for real-time status updates and progress reporting. Include concrete examples of message types, event subscription patterns, and callback mechanisms. Address performance considerations for high-frequency messaging and memory management across language boundaries.

wiki_catalogs.name: Offline Policy Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for QwenEcho's offline policy engine focused on compile-time and runtime network prevention mechanisms. Thoroughly explain the symbol poisoning system preventing accidental network API usage and the security guarantees provided by the air-gapped architecture. Document the policy decision framework that adapts processing quality based on thermal state, memory pressure, and measured latency. Include concrete examples of policy rule implementation, conditional compilation directives, and runtime policy evaluation. Detail the integration with other performance systems including thermal monitor, memory monitor, and latency tracker for holistic resource management. Address policy versioning, configuration management, and testing strategies for offline compliance verification. Provide guidelines for extending policy rules and maintaining offline guarantees during development and deployment.

wiki_catalogs.name: Stage Communication Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed content for QwenEcho's inter-stage communication patterns in the AI processing pipeline. Explain the standardized interface pattern used by ASR, LLM, and TTS stages for consistent data transformation and error propagation. Document the message types (LockedSegment → AsrToLlmElement → LlmToTtsElement → AudioOutput) and their transformation rules at each stage boundary. Detail the asynchronous processing model with queue-based communication and backpressure mechanisms. Include examples of stage initialization, processing callbacks, and cleanup procedures. Address error handling strategies, timeout management, and recovery mechanisms when individual stages fail or become unresponsive. Provide guidance for implementing custom processing stages following the established patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Audio Processing Pipeline
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for QwenEcho's audio processing pipeline focused on real-time speech capture and segmentation. Explain the AudioCollector's platform-independent microphone capture with AAudio (Android) and AVAudioEngine (iOS) integration. Document the lock-free circular ring buffer implementation for zero-contention audio streaming and the SentenceSegmenter's voice activity detection algorithms. Detail the bounded SPSC queue system for producer-consumer communication between pipeline stages. Include audio format handling, sample rate conversion, channel management, and buffer synchronization strategies. Provide examples of configuring audio parameters, handling audio interruptions, and optimizing latency for real-time performance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Warning Overlay System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the WarningOverlay component that handles system alerts and user notifications. Explain the overlay positioning, animation transitions, and dismissible alert patterns. Document the integration with memory monitoring and latency tracking systems to provide proactive warnings about performance issues. Detail the different warning types (memory pressure, thermal throttling, latency spikes) and their corresponding visual representations. Include examples of triggering warnings programmatically, customizing warning appearance, and implementing user acknowledgment flows. Address accessibility considerations, multi-language support, and platform-specific behaviors for iOS and Android.

wiki_catalogs.name: Thread HAL Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Thread HAL component focused on real-time thread management and priority control. Document the thread creation APIs, priority setting mechanisms, and CPU affinity controls. Detail the Android implementation using pthreads with SCHED_FIFO scheduling and the iOS implementation using NSThread with high-priority settings. Include thread naming, stack size configuration, and best practices for real-time audio processing threads.

wiki_catalogs.name: EchoEngine High-Level Facade
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the EchoEngine facade that provides a unified high-level API combining NativeBridge and PortManager functionality. Explain the facade pattern implementation, simplified method signatures, and automatic resource management. Document the complete lifecycle from initialization through pipeline control to cleanup. Include comprehensive examples of typical usage patterns, error handling at the application level, and integration with Flutter widgets. Address configuration options, performance considerations, and best practices for using the facade in production applications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Error Handling and Propagation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for error handling mechanisms focused on how errors propagate through the pipeline stages and are reported to the caller. Explain the error code system defined in echo_types.h and how different error types are handled at various pipeline levels. Document error recovery strategies, retry mechanisms, and graceful degradation patterns. Detail logging and debugging support for error diagnosis. Include examples of handling specific error scenarios and implementing custom error handlers for extended functionality.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Model Processing Stages
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for QwenEcho's AI model processing stages focused on speech recognition, translation, and synthesis. Explain the ASR Stage implementation using FunASR-Nano for 52-language speech-to-text conversion with streaming support. Document the LLM Stage's bilingual translation capabilities using Qwen3-4B-Instruct with context window management and cascade truncation. Detail the TTS Stage's text-to-speech synthesis using Qwen3-TTS-Streaming with real-time audio output. Include stage communication protocols, data structure transformations, and performance optimization strategies. Provide examples of configuring model parameters, handling streaming responses, and integrating additional language packs or model variants.

wiki_catalogs.name: Accelerator HAL Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Accelerator HAL component focused on hardware acceleration support for AI inference. Document the NPU/GPU acceleration interface and model execution optimization APIs. Detail the Android implementation using NNAPI (Neural Networks API) and the iOS implementation using Core ML acceleration backends. Include accelerator availability detection, fallback mechanisms to CPU inference, and performance benchmarking utilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Platform Abstraction Layer (HAL)
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for QwenEcho's Platform Abstraction Layer focused on cross-platform compatibility. Explain the HAL architecture design principles and unified interfaces for platform-specific functionality. Document the Audio HAL implementations using AAudio (Android) and AVAudioEngine (iOS) for microphone capture and speaker output. Detail the Thermal HAL's temperature monitoring across platforms and the Memory HAL's RSS monitoring with progressive mitigation strategies. Include Thread HAL's real-time priority management and Accelerator HAL's NPU/GPU inference support. Provide examples of adding new platform implementations, handling platform differences, and testing cross-platform compatibility.

wiki_catalogs.name: Performance Management Systems
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for QwenEcho's performance management systems focused on adaptive throttling and resource optimization. Explain the three-mode thermal state machine (Normal/Throttle/Critical) with temperature thresholds and corresponding behavior changes. Document the Memory Monitor's progressive cleanup strategies with Level 1 (KV cache release) and Level 2 (pipeline stop) interventions. Detail the Latency Tracker's end-to-end performance monitoring and metrics collection. Include the Offline Policy system's compile-time symbol poisoning and runtime verification to prevent network access. Provide examples of configuring performance budgets, monitoring system health, and implementing custom throttling policies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Model Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for QwenEcho's model management system focused on GGUF model loading and validation. Explain the GGUF/INT4 quantized model format structure and validation procedures. Document the Model Loader's file parsing, verification, and memory-mapped loading mechanisms. Detail the Context Management system's dynamic context window adjustment for performance optimization. Include the Model Catalog's metadata management and version control for model updates. Provide examples of adding new models, validating model integrity, managing model storage, and implementing model update workflows. Address model security, integrity checking, and rollback strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: FFI Bridge and Dart Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for QwenEcho's FFI bridge and Dart integration focused on cross-language communication. Explain the four C-linkage entry points (InitQwenEchoEngine, StartEchoPipeline, StopEchoPipeline, RegisterEchoMessagePort) and their parameter specifications. Document the Dart FFI bindings implementation and error handling strategies for native function calls. Detail the Port Manager's asynchronous message delivery system and the typed event system for inter-process communication. Include the EchoEngine facade's high-level API combining NativeBridge and PortManager functionality. Provide examples of extending the FFI interface, handling async operations, and debugging Dart-native communication issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Build System and Deployment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for QwenEcho's build system and deployment focused on cross-platform compilation and distribution. Explain the CMake configuration for cross-platform native builds with C++17, ARM64 optimization, and RapidCheck testing integration. Document the Android build process with NDK r21+ integration and APK generation. Detail the iOS build configuration using CocoaPods and Xcode project setup for IPA generation. Include Flutter build commands, dependency management, and analysis options configuration. Provide examples of customizing build configurations, setting up continuous integration pipelines, and troubleshooting build issues across different platforms and toolchain versions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for QwenEcho's testing strategy focused on multi-layered quality assurance. Explain the property-based testing approach using RapidCheck for native C++ components with automated test case generation. Document unit testing strategies for individual modules like ASR stage, audio collector, and engine manager. Detail Flutter widget testing for UI components including split view, text display, and status monitoring. Include integration testing approaches for end-to-end pipeline validation and performance benchmarking for latency measurement. Provide examples of writing effective tests, setting up test environments, and interpreting test results across both native and Flutter layers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security and Privacy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for QwenEcho's security and privacy features focused on air-gapped operation guarantees. Explain the offline policy implementation with compile-time symbol poisoning preventing accidental network API usage. Document the runtime verification system that confirms no network libraries are loaded during execution. Detail the application sandbox security model and data isolation strategies ensuring all model files remain within the application boundary. Include privacy guarantees of zero-knowledge architecture where no data leaves the device after model provisioning. Provide examples of verifying offline compliance, auditing network access attempts, and maintaining security boundaries in custom extensions.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for QwenEcho's public interfaces focused on programmatic access to core functionality. Document the four C-linkage FFI functions (InitQwenEchoEngine, StartEchoPipeline, StopEchoPipeline, RegisterEchoMessagePort) with complete parameter specifications, return codes, and usage examples. Detail the EchoErrorCode enumeration and error handling patterns. Include the EchoEngine Dart facade methods for high-level engine control and the complete message protocol for Dart-native communication. Document all data structures, enums, and constants used in the public API. Provide code examples for common integration patterns, error recovery strategies, and best practices for reliable engine usage.

<blog>

# QwenEcho: On-Device Simultaneous Interpretation Engine

## Project Introduction

QwenEcho is a groundbreaking on-device, air-gapped simultaneous interpretation application that runs three AI models entirely offline on mobile hardware. The project eliminates cloud dependencies and network requirements, ensuring complete privacy while providing real-time bilateral translation between two speakers in face-to-face conversations.

### Purpose Statement
QwenEcho enables seamless communication between people speaking different languages by placing a phone between them, where each person sees their speech transcribed while hearing/reading the translation from the other speaker—all processed locally without any data leaving the device.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Complete Privacy**: Zero network requests after model provisioning, with compile-time symbol poisoning preventing accidental network API usage
- **Real-time Performance**: End-to-end latency targets of ≤800ms (Normal) to ≤1200ms (Throttle mode)
- **Multi-language Support**: 52 languages through FunASR-Nano ASR engine
- **On-device Processing**: All three AI models run locally using GGUF/INT4 quantization for efficiency

### Target Audience
- International business professionals requiring private translations
- Diplomatic and government officials needing secure communication tools
- Healthcare providers working with multilingual patients
- Educational institutions facilitating international collaboration

## Technical Architecture

The system follows a layered architecture with clear separation between Flutter UI shell and C/C++ native engine, connected via Dart FFI bindings.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Flutter UI Shell] --> B[Dart FFI Bridge]
    B --> C[Native Port Manager]
    C --> D[C/C++ Native Engine]
    
    D --> E[Audio Collector]
    D --> F[Sentence Segmenter]
    D --> G[ASR Stage]
    D --> H[LLM Stage]
    D --> I[TTS Stage]
    
    E --> J[Ring Buffer]
    F --> K[Bounded SPSC Queue]
    G --> L[Memory Monitor]
    H --> M[Thermal Monitor]
    I --> N[Latency Tracker]
    
    O[Platform HAL] --> E
    O --> I
    
    subgraph "Android/iOS Platform"
        O
    end
```

### Component Breakdown

**Flutter UI Layer**: Provides the bilateral split-screen interface with real-time text display and status monitoring
**Dart FFI Bridge**: Manages communication between Dart and native code through four core entry points
**Native Engine**: Orchestrates the complete audio processing pipeline with lock-free concurrency primitives
**Platform HAL**: Abstracts Android/iOS-specific implementations for audio capture, thermal monitoring, and memory management

### Design Patterns

- **Pipeline Pattern**: Audio flows through sequential stages (Collector → Segmenter → ASR → LLM → TTS)
- **Observer Pattern**: Message-based communication between components via typed event streams
- **State Machine Pattern**: Three-mode thermal management (Normal/Throttle/Critical) with defined transitions
- **Factory Pattern**: Model loading and stage instantiation through centralized managers

### System Relationships

The architecture emphasizes zero-contention performance through lock-free data structures and cascade truncation, where downstream stages begin processing before upstream stages complete. This design enables real-time performance while maintaining data integrity across component boundaries.

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- **lib/main.dart**: Application bootstrap and message routing between EchoEngine and UI components
- **native/include/ffi_bridge.h**: Four C-linkage entry points exposed to Dart FFI
- **lib/qwen_echo.dart**: Barrel exports organizing public API surface

### Core Modules
- **lib/src/echo_engine.dart**: High-level facade combining NativeBridge and PortManager
- **native/include/pipeline_controller.h**: Orchestrates full audio processing pipeline lifecycle
- **native/include/audio_ring_buffer.h**: Lock-free SPSC circular buffer for PCM audio data
- **native/include/bounded_spsc_queue.h**: Overflow-drop bounded queue with sequence protocol

### Configuration Approach
- **pubspec.yaml**: Flutter dependency management with minimal external packages
- **native/CMakeLists.txt**: Cross-platform build configuration supporting Android/iOS
- **native/include/echo_types.h**: Centralized type definitions and engine configuration structure

### External Dependencies
- **FFI Package**: Dart's foreign function interface for native code integration
- **Path Provider**: Local file system access for model provisioning
- **File Picker**: User interface for selecting local model files
- **CMake**: Cross-platform build system with RapidCheck testing framework

### Integration Points
- **HAL Abstraction Layer**: Platform-specific implementations for audio, thermal, and memory monitoring
- **Model Loading Interface**: GGUF format support for quantized AI models
- **Message Protocol**: Typed event system for inter-component communication

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class EchoEngine {
        +state: EchoEngineState
        +messages: Stream~EchoMessage~
        +init(asrPath, llmPath, ttsPath)
        +start(srcLang, tgtLang)
        +stop()
        +dispose()
    }
    
    class SplitView {
        +addAsrPartial(speakerId, text)
        +addAsrConfirmed(speakerId, text)
        +addTranslation(speakerId, text)
        +clearAll()
    }
    
    class PipelineController {
        +create(): PipelineController*
        +start(pc, src_lang, tgt_lang): int
        +stop(pc): int
        +destroy(pc): void
    }
    
    class AudioRingBuffer {
        +write(samples, count): uint32_t
        +read(dest, count): uint32_t
        +available(): uint32_t
    }
    
    EchoEngine --> SplitView : manages
    EchoEngine --> PipelineController : orchestrates
    PipelineController --> AudioRingBuffer : uses
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview
- **Bilateral Translation**: Real-time translation between two speakers with independent text displays
- **Multi-language Support**: 52 languages through FunASR-Nano with context-aware translation
- **Offline Operation**: Complete air-gapped functionality with no network dependencies
- **Thermal Management**: Adaptive performance based on device temperature monitoring
- **Memory Optimization**: Intelligent resource management with automatic cache release

### Implementation Highlights
- **Lock-free Concurrency**: Custom ring buffer and queue implementations eliminate contention bottlenecks
- **Cascade Truncation**: Downstream stages begin processing before upstream completion for reduced latency
- **Three-mode Thermal State Machine**: Automatic performance scaling based on temperature thresholds
- **Platform Abstraction**: Unified API across Android and iOS with optimized native implementations

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Uninitialized
    Uninitialized --> Initializing: InitQwenEchoEngine()
    Initializing --> Ready: Models loaded successfully
    Initializing --> Error: Model load failure
    Ready --> Running: StartEchoPipeline()
    Running --> Stopping: StopEchoPipeline()
    Stopping --> Ready: Graceful shutdown complete
    Error --> Uninitialized: Reset
    
    state Running {
        [*] --> Normal
        Normal --> Throttle: Temperature > 43°C
        Throttle --> Critical: Temperature > 50°C
        Critical --> Throttle: Temperature ≤ 45°C
        Throttle --> Normal: Temperature ≤ 42°C
    }
```

### Performance Targets
The system maintains strict latency budgets across all processing stages:
- **ASR First Character**: ≤200ms (Normal), ≤200ms (Throttle)
- **LLM First Token**: ≤450ms (Normal), ≤450ms (Throttle)  
- **TTS Time-to-first-audio**: ≤100ms (Normal), ≤100ms (Throttle)
- **End-to-end Total**: ≤800ms (Normal), ≤1200ms (Throttle)

### Memory and Thermal Management
Intelligent resource management ensures stable operation under various conditions:
- **Memory Budget**: 2.5GB (Android), 2.0GB (iOS) with progressive cleanup at 85%/95% thresholds
- **Thermal States**: Normal (≤42°C), Throttle (>43°C), Critical (>50°C) with automatic recovery
- **Context Scaling**: Dynamic LLM context window adjustment (512→256 tokens) during throttling

Sources:
- [README.md](README.md)
- [pubspec.yaml](pubspec.yaml)
- [main.dart](lib/main.dart)
- [qwen_echo.dart](lib/qwen_echo.dart)
- [CMakeLists.txt](native/CMakeLists.txt)
- [echo_types.h](native/include/echo_types.h)
- [ffi_bridge.h](native/include/ffi_bridge.h)
- [pipeline_controller.h](native/include/pipeline_controller.h)
- [audio_ring_buffer.h](native/include/audio_ring_buffer.h)
- [bounded_spsc_queue.h](native/include/bounded_spsc_queue.h)
- [split_view.dart](lib/src/ui/split_view.dart)
- [echo_engine.dart](lib/src/echo_engine.dart)

</blog>
